parts.answer: Knowledge 1, Application 2, Analysis 1 Quantitative skills assessed: QS8 Use and interpret quantitative and non-quantitative information in order to make decisions Knowledge Up to 1 mark for giving one reason why product is important e.g. • The product/service needs to meet customer needs (1). Application Up to 2 marks for answer contextualised: • Passengers are looking for luxurious cabins. (1). • Customers expect quality inflight entertainment and food from Emirates. (1). Analysis Up to 1 mark for reasons/causes/consequences for Emirates, e.g. • If the product features are inadequate then customers may turn to competitors, resulting in lower sales and loss of market share for Emirates. (4) Question Answer Mark Knowledge Up to 2 marks for defining social media, e.g. • websites and applications (1) that enable users to create and share content to participate in social networking. (1) OR For stating 2 benefits of social media, e.g. • Builds brand awareness (1) • Gain valuable insights into customer behaviour (1) Application Up to 2 marks for answers contextualised to Emirates e.g. • In a recent study of the top 100 brands most visible on social media, Emirates were ranked fifth after McDonald's, Nike, Adidas and Coca-Cola. (1) • Emirates is the first airline to attract one million followers on the social media site, Instagram (1) Analysis Up to 2 marks for reasons/causes/consequences for Emirates, e.g. • This is likely to increase brand loyalty leading to more demand for Emirates flights and increased profitability. (1). • Emirates can use the information posted on Instagram by customers to monitor the quality of service and take action where required. (1) (6) Question Indicative content Answers must be credited by using the level descriptors (below) in line with the general marking guidance. The indicative content below exemplifies some of the points that candidates may make but this does not imply that any of these must be included. Other relevant points must also
